sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Blast down the start/finish straight at full throttle with DRS open. Brake heavily just after the 150m board, dropping from 8th to 2nd gear. This is one of the heaviest braking zones in F1, so modulate pressure to avoid front lockups. Turn 1 is a tight right; mount the inside curb, but dont overdo it. Quickly flick the car left for Turn 2 — keep it tidy and short-shift on exit to minimise wheelspin. Use the outside kerb to extend the circuit as much as you can, but dont exceed track limits by dipping a wheel into the gravel.

Flat out and flowing. Hug the right side to reduce distance, and build speed as you head toward the next chicane. This is a moment to relax slightly, check ERS mode, and prepare for another heavy braking zone.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake just before the 100m board, down to 3rd gear. The right-left sequence is faster than Turn 1–2 but just as tricky. Use the sausage kerbs with care — too much aggression will destabilise the car. Carry speed through Turn 5 and get a clean exit; it leads straight into a high-speed section. Be careful with the throttle — losing the rear end of your car is very easy to do when getting back on the power.

These fast right-handers are taken in 4th or 5th gear, depending on setup. For Lesmo 1, brake briefly and turn in late — you want a wide entry and early throttle. Avoid the inside kerb like the plague! Use all the exit kerbs. Lesmo 2 is tighter and more technical — brake slightly longer and focus on getting a clean exit for the long DRS zone ahead. Exit traction here is critical for lap time.

Open DRS and stay tight to the right. Managing your ERS here is crucial. If you need help using your ERS, check out our guide here on OverTake! Its a good place to deploy if youre on a push lap or attacking a car in front of you.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
One of the most iconic chicanes in F1. Brake around the 100m board, and downshift to 4th gear. Turn 8 is a fast left flick — use the curb slightly, but dont launch the car over the top of it. Immediately turn right for Turn 9, and then left again for Turn 10. The key here is balance: smooth steering and maintaining stability over the kerbs. A clean exit is vital — you will be at full throttle until you reach the parabolica at the end of the lap.

Monzas final boss: Brake at around the 75m board, down to 4th gear, and turn in aggressively. A late apex is best. Keep it tight in the mid-corner and gradually feed in the throttle — be aggressive on exit without running wide. The exit is DRS detection, so get back on power early for a strong finish to your lap!
